---
title: {studyTitle}
subtitle: Evidence Generation Plan 
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    fontsize: 14pt
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 3
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-title: Contents
    theme: cosmo
    grid:
      body-width: 2200px
      sidebar-width: 200px
      margin-width: 200px
      gutter-width: 50px
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

# About This Document

This Evidence Generation Plan (EGP) defines the analytical experiment that your Picard pipeline will conduct. It specifies:

- **Who** is included in study populations  
- **What** gets measured (outcomes, characteristics, treatments)
- **When** measurements are taken (baseline windows, follow-up periods)
- **How** calculations are performed (numerators, denominators, lookback periods)
- **Why** each choice was made (justification and rationale)

Your Picard code implements these specifications. Every analytical decision should trace back to something documented here. Delete these comments and replace them with your study-specific details.

# Background {#sec-background}

## Rationale {#sec-rationale}

**MODIFY THIS SECTION**

Clearly state why this study matters. What problem does it address? What gap in knowledge will this analysis fill?

Example: "This study characterizes treatment patterns in patients with Type 2 Diabetes to understand the use of guideline-recommended medications and identify opportunities for improved care."

Your rationale here: [WRITE HERE]

## Research Questions {#sec-questions}

**MODIFY THIS SECTION**

State specific, answerable research questions. Frame them in terms of what you're counting or measuring.

Examples:
- "How many patients can we identify with the primary condition of interest?"
- "What is the prevalence of the condition in the study population?"
- "What are the demographic and clinical characteristics of patients at baseline?"
- "What treatment patterns emerge in the study population?"

Your research questions:
[WRITE HERE]

## Data Sources {#sec-data}

**MODIFY THIS SECTION**

Document the database(s) you're using and justify the choice.

Example template:

**Database Name**

- **Time coverage:** [Start date] to [End date]
- **Population size:** [Number of patients/lives]
- **Geography:** [Geographic scope]
- **Data type:** [Claims, EHR, Claims+EHR, etc.]
- **Why chosen:** [Justification - size, coverage, appropriateness for research questions]
- **Known limitations:** [What this database doesn't capture well]

Your data sources:
[WRITE HERE]

# Cohort Definitions {#sec-cohorts}

**MODIFY THIS SECTION - STRUCTURE DEPENDS ON YOUR STUDY DESIGN**

Define how patients enter your study. Be specific about inclusion/exclusion criteria and time windows.

Example for a surveillance/prevalence study:

**Population:** [Name of patient group]

- **Entry criteria:** [When does a patient enter? e.g., "First diagnosis date", "First prescription date"]
- **Inclusion criteria:** [Who stays in? e.g., "365 days continuous enrollment before and after entry"]
- **Exclusion criteria:** [Who is excluded? e.g., "Prior exposure to comparison drug"]
- **Observation window:** [How long are patients followed? e.g., "Until end of data or death"]

Your cohort definitions:
[WRITE HERE]

# Analysis {{#sec-analysis}}

**MODIFY THIS SECTION - STRUCTURE DEPENDS ON YOUR STUDY DESIGN**

Specify exactly what you will measure and how.

## Analysis 1: [Name Your Analysis]

**What is being measured?**
[e.g., "Prevalence of the condition in calendar year 2023"]

**Time windows:**
- Period of interest: [e.g., "Calendar year 2023"]
- Lookback period: [e.g., "365 days before the period"]
- Baseline window: [e.g., "365 days prior to index through 1 day before index"]
- Follow-up window: [e.g., "From index date until end of observation or death"]

**Population specifications:**
- Numerator (What are you counting?): [Be specific. e.g., "Patients with the condition during the period"]
- Denominator (At-risk population): [Be specific. e.g., "All patients with any enrollment during the period"]

**Calculation:**
[Write the formula. e.g., "(Numerator / Denominator) × 100,000 = [Outcome metric]"]

**Stratification (if any):**
[e.g., "By age group, sex, calendar year"]

## Analysis 2: [Name Your Second Analysis]

[Repeat structure above]

[ADD MORE ANALYSES AS NEEDED]

# Appendix

## Links to Implementation (Optional)

Once you complete this EGP, each major section should have a corresponding Picard task:

- Cohort Definitions → `inputs/cohorts/`
- Analysis 1 → `analysis/tasks/01_analysis1.R` (adjust numbering)
- Analysis 2 → `analysis/tasks/02_analysis2.R` (adjust numbering)

Document these mappings as you develop code to ensure consistency between your EGP and implementation.
